title: Convert Decimal to Binary
description: Given a non-negative integer n, return its binary representation without leading zeroes. For this Bit Manipulation track, try to think in terms of reading and shifting bits, not only repeated division.
outputFormat: Return or print the binary representation of n as a string
edgeCases: Large values where many bits are present
intendedApproachSummary: Read the last bit using bitwise logic and move through the number one bit at a time. For example, n & 1 reveals the current least significant bit, and shifting right moves to the next bit.
wrongApproachHints: Using only modulo/division may give the right answer, but it does not practice direct bit access.
If you build digits from right to left, remember to reverse the final result.

examples.explanation: Zero is a special case because the loop for repeated extraction may never run.

title: Check Odd or Even
description: Given an integer n, determine whether it is odd or even. Solve it using a bitwise observation instead of modulo.
outputFormat: Return or print Odd if n is odd, otherwise Even
edgeCases: Very large even and odd values
intendedApproachSummary: The least significant bit decides odd or even. If n & 1 is 1, the number is odd. If it is 0, the number is even.
wrongApproachHints: n % 2 works, but the goal here is to practice reading the last bit directly.
Do not overcomplicate it with loops or string conversion.
hints: Odd numbers end with 1 in binary.

examples.explanation: 7 in binary is 111, so the last bit is 1.
12 in binary is 1100, so the last bit is 0.

title: Check ith Bit
description: Given an integer n and an index i, determine whether the ith bit of n is set. Assume bits are 0-indexed from the right.
inputFormat: An integer i representing a 0-based bit position
outputFormat: Return or print 1 if the ith bit is set, otherwise 0
edgeCases: i larger than the highest set bit of n
intendedApproachSummary: Build a mask using 1 << i and check whether n & mask is non-zero. That directly tests the target bit in constant time.
wrongApproachHints: If you loop through all bits, you are missing the direct mask-based idea.
Be careful about 0-based indexing. The 0th bit is the last bit.

examples.explanation: 10 in binary is 1010, and bit 1 is set.

functionContract.javaSignature: public int checkBit(int n, int index)

title: Set ith Bit
description: Given an integer n and an index i, set the ith bit of n to 1 and return the updated number.
inputFormat: An integer i representing a 0-based bit position
outputFormat: Return the updated integer after setting the ith bit
intendedApproachSummary: Use a mask 1 << i and combine it with n using bitwise OR. OR guarantees the target bit becomes 1.
wrongApproachHints: Do not simulate binary with strings when a mask is enough.
Using XOR here is wrong because XOR toggles instead of always setting.
hints: Use OR to force the bit to 1.

examples.explanation: 8 is 1000 in binary. Setting bit 1 gives 1010, which is 10.

title: Clear ith Bit
description: Given an integer n and an index i, clear the ith bit of n so that the target bit becomes 0.
inputFormat: An integer i representing a 0-based bit position
outputFormat: Return the updated integer after clearing the ith bit
intendedApproachSummary: Create a mask for the ith bit, invert that mask, then AND it with n. This keeps every other bit unchanged and forces the target bit to 0.
wrongApproachHints: Negate the mask, not the number itself.
Missing parentheses around 1 << i often causes the wrong mask.
hints: Build a mask for the ith bit.

examples.explanation: 14 is 1110 in binary. Clearing bit 1 gives 1100, which is 12.

title: Toggle ith Bit
description: Given an integer n and an index i, toggle the ith bit. If the bit is 0 it should become 1, and if it is 1 it should become 0.
inputFormat: An integer i representing a 0-based bit position
outputFormat: Return the updated integer after toggling the ith bit
edgeCases: Toggling a bit that is currently 0
Toggling the same bit twice restores the original value
intendedApproachSummary: Use XOR with a mask 1 << i. XOR flips a bit only when the mask has 1 at that position.
wrongApproachHints: OR only sets a bit, so it cannot be used for toggling.
A loop is unnecessary when one mask and XOR solve it in O(1).
hints: XOR flips when the mask bit is 1.

examples.explanation: 10 is 1010 in binary. Toggling bit 1 changes it to 1000, which is 8.

title: Extract ith Bit Using Right Shift
description: Given an integer n and an index i, extract the ith bit by shifting the number to the right until the target bit reaches the least significant position.
inputFormat: An integer i representing a 0-based bit position
outputFormat: Return or print 1 if the ith bit is set, otherwise 0
edgeCases: Large shift values that move all set bits away
intendedApproachSummary: Shift n right by i places so the target bit becomes the least significant bit, then use & 1 to read it.
wrongApproachHints: Do not forget the final & 1 after shifting.
Shifting the wrong number of times tests the wrong bit.
hints: Shift the target bit to the last position.

examples.explanation: 10 is 1010. Shifting right by 3 gives 1, and 1 & 1 is 1.

title: Count Set Bits with Basic Loop
description: Given a non-negative integer n, count how many bits in its binary representation are set to 1.
outputFormat: Return or print the number of set bits in n
edgeCases: Numbers with a single set bit
Numbers with many consecutive set bits
intendedApproachSummary: Inspect the least significant bit using n & 1, add it to the answer, then right shift n until it becomes 0.
wrongApproachHints: String conversion works, but the intended skill here is bit inspection.
If you forget to shift, the loop never progresses.

examples.explanation: 13 is 1101 in binary, so it has three set bits.

title: Count Set Bits Efficiently
description: Count the number of set bits in n using Brian Kernighan's algorithm. This approach should remove one set bit per iteration.
outputFormat: Return or print the number of set bits in n
intendedApproachSummary: Repeatedly apply n = n & (n - 1). Each iteration removes the lowest set bit, so the loop runs exactly once per set bit.
wrongApproachHints: If you are still shifting through every bit, you have not used the intended optimization.
Remember to increment the counter every time one set bit is removed.
hints: One set bit disappears each time.

examples.explanation: 13 becomes 12, then 8, then 0. Three iterations mean three set bits.

title: Power of Two Check
description: Given an integer n, determine whether it is a power of two. A valid answer must handle zero and negative values correctly.
outputFormat: Return or print true if n is a power of two, otherwise false
intendedApproachSummary: A positive power of two has exactly one set bit. So check n > 0 and then verify (n & (n - 1)) == 0.
wrongApproachHints: A loop that divides by 2 is acceptable mathematically, but it misses the intended bit trick.
Forgetting the n > 0 guard causes 0 to be classified incorrectly.
hints: A power of two has one set bit.

examples.explanation: 16 is 10000 in binary, which contains exactly one set bit.
12 is 1100 in binary, which contains more than one set bit.

title: XOR from 1 to N
description: Given a non-negative integer n, compute the XOR of every integer from 1 through n without iterating through the full range.
outputFormat: Print the XOR of all integers from 1 to n

title: Find Single Number
description: Given an array where every value appears exactly twice except one, find and print the unique value.
outputFormat: Print the value that appears once

title: Find Two Unique Numbers
description: Given an array where exactly two values appear once and every other value appears twice, find the two unique values.
outputFormat: Print the two unique values in ascending order, separated by a space
hints: Find a set bit that differs.

title: Find Missing Number with XOR
description: Given n distinct values chosen from the inclusive range 0 through n, find the one missing value using XOR.
constraints: The array contains n distinct values from 0 through n
outputFormat: Print the missing value

title: Invert All Bits
description: Given a 32-bit signed integer n, invert every bit using the bitwise NOT operator and print the signed result.
outputFormat: Print the signed integer result of bitwise NOT on n

title: Complement Base 10 Integer
description: Given a non-negative integer n, complement only the bits used by its binary representation and print the resulting base-10 value.
outputFormat: Print the base-10 complement of n
edgeCases: Values whose binary form alternates bits

title: Count Odd Numbers in an Array
description: Given an integer array, count how many values are odd by inspecting each value's least significant bit.
outputFormat: Print the number of odd values
hints: Check the last bit of each number.

title: Is ith Bit Set in a Range Query
description: Given an integer n and a zero-based bit index i, print 1 when the ith bit is set and 0 otherwise.
inputFormat: Two space-separated integers n and i
outputFormat: Print 1 if the ith bit is set, otherwise 0
edgeCases: i above the highest set bit

title: Set Multiple Bits from Queries
description: Given an initial integer and q bit-position queries, set every queried bit and print the final integer.
outputFormat: Print the final integer after all set-bit queries
edgeCases: A queried bit is already set
The same position appears more than once

title: Swap Two Numbers Using XOR
description: Given two signed integers a and b, swap their values using XOR and print the swapped pair.
inputFormat: Two space-separated integers a and b
outputFormat: Print the swapped values b and a, separated by a space
hints: Think about aliasing if variables are same.

title: Clear Rightmost Set Bit
description: Given a non-negative integer n, clear its rightmost set bit and print the updated value.
outputFormat: Print n after clearing its rightmost set bit
edgeCases: n is a power of two

title: Toggle Bits in a Given Range
description: Given a non-negative integer n and inclusive zero-based bit positions left and right, toggle every bit in that range.
inputFormat: Three space-separated integers n, left, and right
outputFormat: Print n after toggling every bit from left through right
edgeCases: Every selected bit is initially set
hints: Build masks for each index or a combined mask.

title: Count Bits for All Numbers Up to N
description: Given n, print the number of set bits for every integer from 0 through n using a bit-counting routine.
outputFormat: Print n + 1 space-separated counts for the values 0 through n
edgeCases: n is a power of two

title: Power of Four Check
description: Given an integer n, determine whether it is a power of four.
outputFormat: Print true if n is a power of four, otherwise false
edgeCases: A power of two that is not a power of four
hints: Then ensure the set bit is in the right position.

title: Single Number II
description: Given an array where every value appears three times except one value that appears once, find and print the unique value.
constraints: Every non-unique value appears exactly three times
outputFormat: Print the value that appears once
hints: Numbers appear three times except one.
Count bits or use state masks.

title: Find Repeating and Missing Pair with XOR
description: Given n values from 1 through n where one value repeats and one is missing, recover both values.
constraints: Exactly one value repeats and exactly one is missing
outputFormat: Print the repeating value followed by the missing value

title: Find Missing Numbers in Bitwise Buckets
description: Given n distinct values chosen from 0 through n, recover the missing value using XOR bucket cancellation.
constraints: The array contains n distinct values from 0 through n
outputFormat: Print the missing value

title: Generate All Subsets
hints: Read each bit to decide inclusion.

title: Subset Sum via Bitmask Enumeration
description: Given an array and a target, count the index-distinct subsets whose values sum to the target using bitmask enumeration.
outputFormat: Print the number of subsets whose sum equals target
edgeCases: Equal values at different indices form distinct subsets
hints: Compute the sum for chosen elements.

title: Visited Cities Mask
description: Given q visited city indices, store visits in a bitmask and print the number of distinct cities visited.
outputFormat: Print the number of distinct visited cities
edgeCases: Every visit is to the same city
hints: Set a city bit when visited.
Store everything in one integer mask.

title: Assign Tasks with Availability Mask
hints: Represent chosen workers by a mask.
Transitions add one bit at a time.

title: Hamming Distance
description: Given two non-negative integers x and y, count the bit positions at which they differ.
inputFormat: Two space-separated integers x and y
outputFormat: Print the Hamming distance between x and y
hints: Count the set bits in the XOR value.

title: Number Complement
description: Given a positive integer n, flip every bit in its active binary representation and print the resulting integer.
outputFormat: Print the complement of n using only its active bits

title: Reverse Bits
hints: Shift answer left before adding next bit.

title: Bitwise AND of Numbers Range
description: Given integers left and right, compute the bitwise AND of every integer in the inclusive range.
inputFormat: Two space-separated integers left and right
outputFormat: Print the bitwise AND of all values from left through right
edgeCases: The values share a long binary prefix
hints: Shift both numbers until they match.

title: Sum of Two Integers Without Plus
description: Given two 32-bit signed integers a and b, compute their sum without using the plus or minus operators.
constraints: The result fits in a 32-bit signed integer
inputFormat: Two space-separated integers a and b
outputFormat: Print the sum of a and b

title: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
description: Given an array of non-negative integers, find the maximum XOR obtainable from any pair of values.
outputFormat: Print the maximum XOR of any pair
edgeCases: The best pair uses high-order bits
hints: Build the answer bit by bit.
Test whether a candidate bit can be set.

title: Decode XORed Array
description: Given an XOR-encoded array and its first decoded value, reconstruct and print the original array.
inputFormat: An integer n representing the encoded array length
outputFormat: Print the n + 1 decoded values as space-separated integers
hints: XOR helps recover the next element.

title: Count Bits DP
description: Given n, use the recurrence count[i] = count[i >> 1] + (i & 1) to print set-bit counts from 0 through n.
outputFormat: Print n + 1 space-separated counts for the values 0 through n
edgeCases: n is a power of two
hints: Add i & 1 to extend the count.

title: Minimum Bit Flips to Convert Number
description: Given non-negative integers start and goal, return the minimum number of individual bit flips required to convert start into goal.
inputFormat: Two space-separated integers start and goal
outputFormat: Print the minimum number of required bit flips
hints: Every set bit is one flip.
